varieties.name: Banksia integrifolia
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (Victoria to Central Queensland), collected 1770
varieties.traits: Highly variable tree/shrub, 2-25m tall (can reach 35m in sheltered locations). Dark green leaves with white undersides create striking contrast in wind. Bark fissured or tessellated.
varieties.availability: Widely available from Queensland and Australian native plant nurseries. One of the most commonly cultivated species.

varieties.name: Banksia dentata
varieties.origin: Northern Australia (Cooktown to Kimberley), Papua New Guinea, Aru Islands. Collected 1770.
varieties.traits: Gnarled tree to 7m high. Large green leaves up to 22cm long with dentate (toothed) margins. Only Banksia species not endemic to Australia.
varieties.availability: Available from specialist Australian native nurseries, particularly in Queensland.

varieties.name: Banksia robur
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (NSW, Queensland), collected 1770 at Botany Bay
varieties.traits: Large shrub to small tree, 2-5m tall. Highly variable species with diverse color forms even from same seed stock. Grows in sandy soils and swampy sites, prefers constant water access.
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland nurseries including Yuruga Nursery (Mount Windsor seed stock).

varieties.name: Banksia aemula
varieties.origin: Subtropical east coast (mid-Queensland to mid-NSW)
varieties.traits: Sprawly gnarly tree found in beach dunes, coastal areas, and wallum (sandy soils). Brown bark (unlike grey bark of B. serrata). Deep roots access water despite sandy habitat.
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland and NSW coastal region nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia aquilonia
varieties.origin: North Queensland (Crystal Creek to Ingham region), described 1981
varieties.traits: Tall shrub/small tree to 8m (occasionally 15m). Narrow glossy green leaves up to 20cm long with distinctive reddish-brown hairs on midrib underside.
varieties.availability: Uncommon in cultivation but becoming more available. Specialist Queensland nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia plagiocarpa
varieties.origin: Hinchinbrook Island and adjacent North Queensland mainland (Mt Bowen, Nina Peak, Bishops Peak). First collected 1867, described 1981.
varieties.traits: Shrub to 5m high with greyish broken bark. New growth covered in red velvety fur. Narrow lanceolate leaves 8-20cm long.
varieties.availability: Initially rare but rapidly becoming more common in cultivation. Queensland specialist nurseries. Used for cut flower production in coastal NSW and SE Queensland.

varieties.name: Banksia spinulosa
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (Victoria to Queensland, extending to Cairns). First named 1793.
varieties.traits: Multi-stemmed shrub 1-3m tall (can reach 5m as single-stemmed tree). Has lignotuber for fire regeneration. Long thin leaves, highly variable flower style colors (black, purple, red, orange, yellow, gold).

varieties.name: Banksia serrata
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (southern Victoria through NSW to central Queensland). Collected 1770.
varieties.traits: Large shrub to tree 10-15m tall. Often gnarly, lumpy, twisted form. Grey tessellated bark. Dark green leaves white beneath. Short and compact in exposed coastal sites, larger in sheltered gardens.
varieties.availability: Widely available from Queensland and Eastern Australian nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia ericifolia
varieties.origin: Central and Northern NSW east of Great Dividing Range (two separate regions). Collected 1770. Official flower of Sydney.
varieties.traits: Medium to large shrub 2-6m high (1-2m in exposed coastal areas). Small slender heather-like leaves. Dense growth habit.

varieties.name: Banksia oblongifolia
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (mid-Queensland to mid-NSW)
varieties.traits: Big bushy Banksia found in sandy soils, swampy sites, clay, loam and granite. Grows in forest, bushland and beachfront with access to water.
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland and NSW native plant nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia paludosa
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (coastal and ranges from Sydney to Victorian border)
varieties.traits: Small shrub to 2m with whorled leaves. Grows on dry rocky acid soil in open spaces. Perfect size for pots and containers.
varieties.availability: Not often found in gardens, but available from specialist Australian native nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia marginata
varieties.origin: Southeastern Australia (Eyre Peninsula SA to Baradine NSW, throughout Victoria and Tasmania). One report from Springbrook Mountains near Brisbane.
varieties.traits: Highly variable: 20cm shrub to 12m tree. Most often 2m shrub. Upper leaf surface dark green, lower surface white-tomentose creating silvery appearance in wind. Most widely distributed Banksia species.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Birthday Candles'
varieties.origin: Schnapper Point near Ulladulla, NSW (exposed headland). Developed by Bill Molyneux, Austraflora. Plant breeder rights granted 1989.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Cherry Candles'
varieties.origin: Schnapper Point near Ulladulla, NSW. Released by Austraflora 2004.
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf 50cm high x 75-90cm wide. Dense, flat growth habit. Flower spikes nearly twice as tall as 'Birthday Candles'.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Honey Pots'
varieties.origin: Selection from Banksia spinulosa
varieties.traits: Dwarf shrub 1m high x 1-1.5m wide. Dense compact growth. Rugged leaves with bright golden color when young.
varieties.availability: Widely available. Sunshine Coast nurseries, Queensland and Australian-wide retailers.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Coastal Cushion'
varieties.origin: Schnapper Point, NSW. Collected by Neil Marriott, propagated by Richard Anderson.
varieties.traits: Low groundcover 60cm high with twice the spread of 'Birthday Candles' and 'Cherry Candles'.
varieties.availability: Available from specialist native plant nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Stumpy Gold'
varieties.origin: Selection from Hairpin Banksia
varieties.traits: Low growing dwarf form with mounded growth habit
varieties.availability: Available from Australian native plant nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Giant Candles'
varieties.origin: Originated at Brisbane garden (spontaneous hybrid). Propagated and registered by Russell Costin, Limpinwood Nursery, 1970s.
varieties.traits: Large shrub to 5m tall x 5m wide. Can be pruned hard and resprouts from lignotuber. Grows 800mm/year in ideal conditions.
varieties.availability: Widely available. Popular in Queensland and Eastern Australia. May need pruning for space-limited gardens.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Little Eric'
varieties.origin: Arose as chance garden seedling. Propagated by Richard Anderson, Merricks Nursery (Mornington Peninsula, VIC).
varieties.traits: Dwarf compact form 1.5-2m high x 1-1.5m wide. Dense habit. Excellent for small gardens.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Bulli Baby'
varieties.origin: NSW coastal selection
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf 1.5m high x 1.2m wide. Extremely floriferous - 50-60 flower heads per season on mature plants.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Bronzed Aussie'
varieties.origin: Unknown origin. Propagated by Rod Parsons, Carawah Nursery. Seed given to Rod's father by SGAP member many years ago, propagated vegetatively since.
varieties.traits: Medium shrub to 2m high. Unique bud clustering at base of main flower.
varieties.availability: Available from Carawah Nursery and select retailers.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Roller Coaster'
varieties.origin: Coastal NSW selection. Also marketed as 'Austraflora Roller Coaster'.
varieties.traits: Vigorous prostrate groundcover spreading 4-5m wide but remaining only 50cm high. Excellent coastal tolerance.
varieties.availability: Registered cultivar, available from Australian native plant nurseries.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Pygmy Possum'
varieties.origin: Dwarf coastal form of Saw Banksia
varieties.traits: Prostrate groundcover with spreading growth. Low-growing form of normally 5-6m upright species.

varieties.name: Banksia 'Mini Marg'
varieties.origin: Selected compact form of Silver Banksia
varieties.traits: Compact shrub form, much smaller than typical 2-12m species range

varieties.name: Banksia 'Yellow Wing'
varieties.origin: Deliberately hybridized by Austraflora
varieties.traits: Similar size to 'Coastal Cushion'. Named for frequent visits by New Holland honeyeaters.
varieties.availability: Available from Austraflora and select retailers.

varieties.name: Banksia coccinea (Scarlet Banksia) ⚠️ NOT SUITABLE
varieties.origin: SW Western Australia (Denmark to Stirling Range)

varieties.name: Banksia baxteri (Bird's Nest Banksia) ⚠️ NOT SUITABLE
varieties.origin: SW Western Australia (Albany to Stirling Range)

varieties.name: Banksia prionotes (Acorn Banksia) ⚠️ NOT SUITABLE
varieties.origin: SW Western Australia
